Patch Set 1:

This is wrong and breaks the Native Linux client. We need another way to fix things.
Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> This is wrong and breaks the Native Linux client. We need another way to fix things.

Okay.

Currently I am seeing this:

/home/shaun/lustre-release/libcfs/include/libcfs/util/string.h:45:10: fatal error: linux/lnet/lnet-types.h: No such file or directory
   45 | #include <linux/lnet/lnet-types.h>
      |          ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
compilation terminated.

Which suggests that we need the uapi includes?

I'll see what I can figure out ...

Patch Set 1:

Build Failed 

https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/95769/ : FAILURE

Patch Set 1:

So you are disabling building the modules with a kernel without native lustre client support?

Patch Set 1:

The purpose of the current way is that its possible that headers in /usr/include/linux and / or the kernel headers would conflict with the OpenSFS headers. We need a way to test if the lustre headers are in the kernel source tree or /usr/include/linux.

Patch Set 1:

(3 comments)

So how to the libcfs uapi headers get picked up?
Are they part of in-kernel lustre?

commit.subject: LU-16918 build: Restore --disable-modules support
commit.message:
LU-16918 build: Restore --disable-modules support

Support for --disable-modules was lost a while back. Other less
frequently used build combinations were also impacted.

Prefer the installed kernel headers if they suggest lustre
support is available, otherwise use the kernel headers provided
from the local libcfs tree.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>
Change-Id: I730c506d7cc8d29f4cbc8168fd27dd1e1bc5eb68
